name: Marzipan (Almond Paste)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place almond paste in a bowl and knead.
2. Add egg white and mix well.
3. Gradually add powdered sugar until the consistency is that of very soft dough.
4. Add Karo syrup and lemon flavoring.
5. Add enough sugar so that the dough is sticky but does not stick to your fingers.
6. Keep covered with a damp cloth.
7. Color the marzipan to suit the fruit or vegetable you plan to make.
8. Roll into a 3/4 inch diameter and cut into 1 1/4-inch pieces.
9. Dust with powdered sugar to prevent sticking to board and fingers.
Pears: Yellow - form, add a whole clove at the bottom. Fleck lightly with brown.
Peaches: Yellow - form, crease one side with a knife blade. Shade red.
Bananas: Yellow - form, fleck lightly with brown. Shade end lightly with green.
Oranges: Orange - form, roll to resemble orange skins using a sieve.
Carrots: Orange - form, crease at intervals with a knife blade.
Apples: Delicate green - form, indent the top. Add a whole clove at the bottom. Shade red; optionally add an artificial leaf at the top stem end.
Strawberries: Red - form, indent the top, roll in red sugar.
New Potatoes: Uncolored marzipan - form, press "eyes" into each potato. Roll in sugar mixed with cocoa.
Watermelon: Form a thin oval of green, a thin oval of white (same size). Place the white over the green. Form an oblong roll of red and place it between the green and white ovals. Let dry. Slice and add black food coloring "seeds" using a toothpick.
NOTE: Do not use liquid food coloring from the supermarket. Use cake decorator coloring that comes in a small jar, available at Wilton's or other cake decorator suppliers.
